node.name: PSYCHOLOGY
node.rich_text:
Psych-Behavioral Game Design Hexcelerate by Hexcelerate 0 •
Game Development Validates game design patterns using behavioral psychology frameworks to ensure ethical engagement and effective learning.
About SKILL.md FAQ This skill integrates behavioral science into the game development lifecycle by applying established frameworks like the Fogg Behavior Model, Hook Model, and Nudge Theory. It provides developers with a structured way to validate game mechanics, ensuring they respect player wellbeing, promote healthy habit formation, and optimize learning paths. It is particularly useful for developers building educational or age-appropriate web games who need to balance engagement with ethical design boundaries.
Key Features 01 0 GitHub stars 02 Implements ethical Hook Model validation to ensure engagement serves learning goals 03 Utilizes Nudge Theory to make educational paths the default player experience 04 Applies the Fogg Behavior Model (B=MAT) to analyze motivation, ability, and triggers 05 Provides a comprehensive validation checklist for player attention and progress saving 06 Analyzes session design for age-appropriate durations and natural pause points Use Cases 01 Designing ethical retention loops for educational web games 02 Validating tutorial flows to ensure players are effectively nudged toward learning 03 Auditing game sessions for age-appropriate play durations (ages 6-8)
Validates that game design creates healthy behavioral patterns and respects player attention and wellbeing.
Behavior requires all three at the same moment:
— Player can do it (it's easy enough) •
— Something prompts the action (clear call to action)
◇ Investment makes the next trigger more engaging ◇ ETHICAL BOUNDARY: Hooks must serve the player's learning goals, not extract attention for its own sake
Design defaults that lead to learning:
◇ Default paths should be educational (not skip-to-fun) ◇ Make the learning path the easiest path ◇ Don't hide educational content behind extra clicks
Give players a reason to come back ◇
End sessions at a compelling moment ◇
◇ Ages 6-8: ~12-18 minutes per session ◇ Natural pause points every 3-5 minutes ◇ Clear session boundaries (start → middle → end) ◇ Save progress automatically

◇ Next action is always clear and easy (high ability) ◇ Default paths are educational (nudge) ◇ Session length respects attention span (~12-18 min for ages 6-8) ◇ Return motivation exists without dark patterns ◇ Natural pause points every 3-5 minutes ◇ Progress is automatically saved ◇ Trigger → Action → Reward cycle serves learning goals ◇ No attention-extracting mechanics

row.cell: Behavioral design validation for game development. Invoked by game-psychologist agent at validation checkpoints. Applies Fogg Behavior Model, Hook Model (Nir Eyal), Nudge Theory, Habit Formation, and Session Design to validate behavioral patterns.
